name: Protalix BioTherapeutics, Inc.
summary: Protalix BioTherapeutics is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on discovery, development, production, and commercialization of therapeutics for rare diseases. It uses its proprietary ProCellEx plant cell-based protein expression system. Its approved products include Elelyso (taliglucerase alfa) for Gaucher disease and Elfabrio (pegunigalsidase alfa) for Fabry disease. Pipeline candidates include PRX-115 for uncontrolled gout and PRX-119 for NETs-related diseases.

products.name: Elelyso (taliglucerase alfa)
products.description: Recombinant glucocerebrosidase enzyme replacement therapy approved by FDA in 2012 for Gaucher disease in adults and children four years and older; approved in 40+ markets.

products.name: Elfabrio (pegunigalsidase alfa-iwxj)
products.description: PEGylated recombinant alpha-Galactosidase-A enzyme replacement therapy approved by FDA and EC in May 2023 for adult Fabry disease patients.

products.name: PRX-115
products.description: PEGylated uricase in Phase 2 clinical trial (RELEASE study) for treatment of uncontrolled gout.

products.name: PRX-119
products.description: Long-acting DNaseI in development for the treatment of NETs-related diseases.

products.name: ProCellEx
products.description: Proprietary plant cell-based protein expression system used to develop recombinant proteins at industrial scale.

partnerships.description: Exclusive license and supply agreement granting Pfizer worldwide commercialization rights to Elelyso (excluding Brazil) through 2030.
Two exclusive global licensing and supply agreements for development and commercialization of Elfabrio for Fabry disease, covering US and ex-US markets.
Supply and Technology Transfer Agreement signed June 2013 granting Fiocruz rights to commercialize Elelyso in Brazil as BioManguinhos alfataliglicerase.
